name: Julekake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, yeast, and ground cardamom.
2. In a saucepan, heat milk, butter or margarine, sugar, and salt just until warm (115-120°F). Stir constantly.
3. Add to the dry mixture in the mixing bowl; add egg.
4. Beat on low speed of an electric mixer for 1/2 minute, scraping sides of the bowl constantly.
5. Beat at high speed for 3 minutes.
6. By hand, stir in mixed candied fruits and light raisins.
7. Stir in enough flour to make a soft dough.
8. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface; knead until smooth (8-10 minutes).
9. Place in a greased bowl; turn once. Cover. Let rise until doubled (1 ¼ - 1 ½ hours).
10. Punch down. Divide into two. Cover and let rest for 10 minutes.
11. Shape into 2 loaves. Place in two 9x5x3-inch loaf pans. Cover. Let rise until doubled (about 45 minutes).
12. Combine beaten egg yolk and water; brush over loaves.
13. Bake at 350°F for 35-40 minutes. Remove from pans. Cool. Drizzle with confectioners' icing. Trim with almonds and candied cherries.
